---
title: "Environment"
description: "CO2 emissions, energy, and environmental indicators — Canada and OECD peers"
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
import os
from pipeline.config import (
    CANADA_COLOR, PEER_COLOR, HIGHLIGHT_WIDTH, PEER_WIDTH,
    HIGHLIGHT_COUNTRY, PEER_COUNTRIES, get_data_date,
)
from pipeline.charts import peer_comparison_line, ranked_bar
```

How much carbon Canada emits, how that has changed over time, and how its energy is sourced — shown against OECD peers. Production-based measures count emissions where they physically occur.

## CO2 Emissions per Capita

Production-based CO2 emissions per person, in tonnes of CO2. This is the most directly comparable measure of a country's carbon footprint, controlling for population size.

```{python}
#| echo: false
co2cap_path = "data/environment/oecd_co2_per_capita.csv"
if os.path.exists(co2cap_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(co2cap_path)

    fig = peer_comparison_line(
        df, x_col="year", y_col="co2_per_capita",
        title="", yaxis_title="Tonnes CO2 per capita",
    )
    fig.update_layout(
        annotations=[dict(
            text=f"Source: OECD Green Growth Indicators | Data as of: {get_data_date(co2cap_path)}",
            xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.52,
            showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"),
        )],
    )
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("CO2 per capita data not yet available. Run the data pipeline to fetch it.")
```

## Latest CO2 per Capita: Ranked

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(co2cap_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(co2cap_path)
    fig = ranked_bar(df, "co2_per_capita", "Tonnes CO2 per capita",
        f"Source: OECD Green Growth Indicators | Data as of: {get_data_date(co2cap_path)}",
        hover_template="%{y}: %{x:.1f} t CO2/capita<extra></extra>")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False})
```

## CO2 Emissions Trend (Indexed, 2000 = 100)

How each country's CO2 emissions have changed relative to 2000 levels. Values below 100 indicate reductions since 2000; above 100 indicate growth.

```{python}
#| echo: false
co2idx_path = "data/environment/oecd_co2_indexed.csv"
if os.path.exists(co2idx_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(co2idx_path)

    fig = peer_comparison_line(
        df, x_col="year", y_col="co2_index",
        title="", yaxis_title="Index (2000 = 100)",
    )
    # Add reference line at 100
    fig.add_hline(y=100, line_dash="dash", line_color="#888", opacity=0.5)
    fig.update_layout(
        annotations=[dict(
            text=f"Source: OECD Green Growth Indicators | Data as of: {get_data_date(co2idx_path)}",
            xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.52,
            showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"),
        )],
    )
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("CO2 indexed data not yet available. Run the data pipeline to fetch it.")
```

## Renewable Energy Share

Renewable energy as a percentage of total primary energy supply. Canada has significant hydroelectric capacity, which shows in its renewable share.

```{python}
#| echo: false
renew_path = "data/environment/oecd_renewables_share.csv"
if os.path.exists(renew_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(renew_path)

    fig = peer_comparison_line(
        df, x_col="year", y_col="renewables_pct",
        title="", yaxis_title="% of total energy supply",
    )
    fig.update_layout(
        annotations=[dict(
            text=f"Source: OECD Green Growth Indicators | Data as of: {get_data_date(renew_path)}",
            xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.52,
            showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"),
        )],
    )
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Renewable energy data not yet available. Run the data pipeline to fetch it.")
```

## Latest Renewable Energy Share: Ranked

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(renew_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(renew_path)
    fig = ranked_bar(df, "renewables_pct", "% of total energy supply",
        f"Source: OECD Green Growth Indicators | Data as of: {get_data_date(renew_path)}",
        hover_template="%{y}: %{x:.1f}%<extra></extra>")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False})
```

## Energy Mix by Country

Primary energy supply broken down by source. Shows the share of each country's energy that comes from fossil fuels (coal, oil, gas), nuclear, and renewables. Countries are sorted by renewable energy share.

```{python}
#| echo: false
emix_path = "data/environment/owid_energy_mix.csv"
if os.path.exists(emix_path):
    emix = pd.read_csv(emix_path)
    latest_year = emix.dropna(subset=["coal_share_energy"]).groupby("country_code")["year"].max()

    # Get latest available year per country
    rows = []
    for cc, yr in latest_year.items():
        row = emix[(emix["country_code"] == cc) & (emix["year"] == yr)]
        if not row.empty:
            rows.append(row.iloc[0])
    latest = pd.DataFrame(rows)

    # Sort by renewables share (highest at top)
    latest = latest.sort_values("renewables_share_energy", ascending=True)

    # Energy source categories and colors
    sources = [
        ("coal_share_energy", "Coal", "#4a4a4a"),
        ("oil_share_energy", "Oil", "#8c564b"),
        ("gas_share_energy", "Natural Gas", "#ff7f0e"),
        ("nuclear_share_energy", "Nuclear", "#9467bd"),
        ("renewables_share_energy", "Renewables", "#2ca02c"),
    ]

    fig = go.Figure()
    for col, name, color in sources:
        vals = latest[col].fillna(0)
        fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
            y=latest["country_name"],
            x=vals,
            name=name,
            orientation="h",
            marker_color=color,
            hovertemplate=f"{name}: %{{x:.1f}}%<extra>%{{y}}</extra>",
        ))

    fig.update_layout(
        barmode="stack",
        xaxis_title="% of primary energy supply",
        yaxis_title="",
        plot_bgcolor="white",
        xaxis=dict(gridcolor="#e0e0e0", range=[0, 100]),
        legend=dict(
            orientation="h", yanchor="top", y=-0.08,
            xanchor="center", x=0.5,
        ),
        height=650,
        margin=dict(b=120, t=20),
        annotations=[dict(
            text=f"Source: Our World in Data (Energy Institute) | Data as of: {get_data_date(emix_path)}",
            xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.22,
            showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"),
        )],
    )
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Energy mix data not yet available. Run the data pipeline to fetch it.")
```

## Canada's Energy Mix Over Time

How Canada's energy sources have shifted over the decades.

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(emix_path):
    canada_emix = emix[emix["country_code"] == "CAN"].copy()
    canada_emix = canada_emix.dropna(subset=["coal_share_energy"])
    canada_emix = canada_emix.sort_values("year")

    sources = [
        ("coal_share_energy", "Coal", "#4a4a4a"),
        ("oil_share_energy", "Oil", "#8c564b"),
        ("gas_share_energy", "Natural Gas", "#ff7f0e"),
        ("nuclear_share_energy", "Nuclear", "#9467bd"),
        ("renewables_share_energy", "Renewables", "#2ca02c"),
    ]

    fig = go.Figure()
    for col, name, color in sources:
        fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
            x=canada_emix["year"],
            y=canada_emix[col].fillna(0),
            name=name,
            mode="lines",
            stackgroup="one",
            line=dict(width=0.5, color=color),
            fillcolor=color,
            hovertemplate=f"{name}: %{{y:.1f}}%<extra></extra>",
        ))

    fig.update_layout(
        xaxis_title="",
        yaxis_title="% of primary energy supply",
        yaxis=dict(range=[0, 100], gridcolor="#e0e0e0"),
        xaxis=dict(gridcolor="#e0e0e0"),
        plot_bgcolor="white",
        legend=dict(
            orientation="h", yanchor="top", y=-0.08,
            xanchor="center", x=0.5,
        ),
        margin=dict(b=120, t=20),
        annotations=[dict(
            text=f"Source: Our World in Data (Energy Institute) | Data as of: {get_data_date(emix_path)}",
            xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.22,
            showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"),
        )],
    )
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Energy mix data not yet available. Run the data pipeline to fetch it.")
```

## Download Data

- [CO2 per Capita (CSV)](../data/environment/oecd_co2_per_capita.csv) — OECD Green Growth Indicators
- [CO2 Emissions Indexed (CSV)](../data/environment/oecd_co2_indexed.csv) — OECD Green Growth Indicators
- [Renewable Energy Share (CSV)](../data/environment/oecd_renewables_share.csv) — OECD Green Growth Indicators
- [Energy Mix (CSV)](../data/environment/owid_energy_mix.csv) — Our World in Data (Energy Institute)
